expo_desc.title: Egypt Through the Lens of the 9th Art
expo_desc.p1: In the language of the pyramid builders, “Kheti” is the closest word to what we now call comics.
expo_desc.p2: For nearly a century, the mysteries of the sand and the tombs have fuelled the imagination of the greatest comic artists. From Edgar P. Jacobs’ The Mystery of the Great Pyramid to the cult parodies of Asterix, join us on a visual journey through time.
expo_desc.p3: Let yourself drift along and watch a paper myth evolve.

items.title: Cigars of the pharaoh
items.catchphrase: Egypt as a mysterious backdrop (stereotypical settings)
items.content_1: In The Cigars of the Pharaoh, Hergé portrays Egypt as a mysterious and exotic place. Inspired by the discovery of Tutankhamun’s tomb in 1922, the album depicts an Egypt filled with pyramids, tombs, and mummies.
items.content_2: The architecture and tombs serve primarily as an adventure backdrop, complete with secret passages and hidden locations tied to secret societies — as when Tintin explores the tomb of Kih-Oskh, surrounded by rows of mummies.

items.title: The Mystery of the Great Pyramid
items.catchphrase: Fantastical Egypt and the myth of the curse
items.content_1: In The Mystery of the Great Pyramid by Edgar P. Jacobs, Egypt is depicted with remarkable realism, featuring detailed views of Cairo, the Giza plateau, the Sphinx, and the pyramids.
items.content_2: The architecture feels sacred and monumental, setting the modern city against the silence of the pyramids, which are presented as ancient places steeped in mystery and spirituality — most notably through the “Chamber of Horus”.

items.title: Asterix and Cleopatra
items.content_1: In Asterix and Cleopatra by René Goscinny and Albert Uderzo, set in 48 BC, Egypt is brought to life through its iconic landmarks — palaces, pyramids, and the Sphinx.
items.content_2: The buildings are drawn in a spectacular and vibrant style, presenting a grand and monumental Egypt, while construction sites and building projects are used to comedic effect throughout.

items.title: Papyrus
items.catchphrase: Egypt as a grand political stage
items.content_1: In Papyrus by Lucien De Gieter, set in the Egypt of the ancient pharaohs, the country is portrayed as a mythological world rooted in the beliefs of ancient Egyptian civilisation.
items.content_2: Animal-headed gods, fantastical creatures, and magic are ever-present, painting Egypt as a land of legend where the gods intervene directly in human affairs.

items.title: The Prince of the Nile
items.catchphrase: Egypt as a centre of power and intrigue
items.content_1: In The Prince of the Nile by Jacques Martin, set in the Ptolemaic period around 51 BC, Egypt is depicted as a wealthy and powerful kingdom defined by the opulence of its palaces, jewellery, and costumes.
items.content_2: The country also emerges as a hotbed of power struggles and conspiracies surrounding the pharaoh’s throne.

items.title: Indiana Jones
items.catchphrase: Egypt as a cinematic adventure setting
items.content_1: In The Cigars of the Pharaoh, Hergé portrays Egypt as a mysterious and exotic place. Inspired by the discovery of Tutankhamun’s tomb in 1922, the album depicts an Egypt filled with pyramids, tombs, and mummies.
items.content_2: The architecture and tombs serve primarily as an adventure backdrop, complete with secret passages and hidden locations tied to secret societies — as when Tintin explores the tomb of Kih-Oskh, surrounded by rows of mummies.

labyrinthe.title: The maze
labyrinthe.desc_1: Do you dare take on the challenge? Test your sense of direction and your logic by guiding a ball to the exit. But be warned… some paths lead to dead ends! Choose the right turn, think fast, and find your way out as quickly as you can.
labyrinthe.desc_2: And that’s not all! Players who complete the challenge will be able to take home a free gift. All the more reason to give it a go! Ready to play? Step into the maze and find out if you’re a true explorer at heart!

mentions_legales.title: Legal notice
mentions_legales.intro: This legal notice is provided as part of the Kheti educational project. It describes the publishing, hosting, and personal data processing information relating to the use of this website.

editor.title: Website publisher
editor.p1: The Kheti website is published as part of a student project from the BUT MMI programme (Multimedia and Internet Professions) at Université Gustave Eiffel.
editor.director: Publication director: Gilles Roussel, President of Université Gustave Eiffel.

team.title: Production team
team.p1: The Faravision agency is the creator of the Kheti exhibition. This website was designed and developed by a BUT MMI student team.

hosting.title: Hosting
hosting.p1: This website is hosted by OVH SAS.

privacy.title: Personal data protection (GDPR)
privacy.p1: When using the Kheti website, personal data may be collected (last name, first name, email address, booking information). This data is used solely for the operation of the service (account management, bookings).
privacy.p2: This data is neither sold nor passed on to third parties for commercial purposes.

dpo.title: Data Protection Officer (DPO)
dpo.p1: For any questions regarding the processing of your personal data:
dpo.p2: In accordance with the GDPR, you have the right to access, correct, and delete your data through your profile page or by contacting us directly.

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.p1: This website uses only technical cookies that are strictly necessary for it to function properly.
cookies.p2: No advertising or profiling cookies are used.

credits.title: Credits and intellectual property
credits.p1: This website is a non-profit educational project.
list: The original text, visuals, logos, and graphic elements of the Kheti project are protected by copyright.
Any reproduction, distribution, or reuse without prior written authorisation is prohibited, except where permitted by law.
The names, trademarks, and logos of third parties mentioned on this website remain the property of their respective owners and are used solely for informational and educational purposes.

intro.title: The Maze Roller
intro.p1: Your goal is to guide the ball through the corridors of this maze and find the way out. Use your sense of direction and logic to overcome the dead ends that lie ahead.
intro.p2: Complete all levels to unlock an exclusive reward, available to collect during your visit to the exhibition.
intro.controls_touch: Swipe your finger across the screen to move.
intro.controls_keyboard: Use the arrow keys or "ZQSD" to move.
